settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: These settings are stored in your browser and kept across visits.
settings.friendlyLabelsHint: Show clear names like “Air temperature” instead of technical ids.
settings.explorerModeHint: Which mode the Explorer opens in.
settings.convertUnitsHint: Convert values into the units you choose.

support.title: Support
support.reportText: Found a bug? Please open an issue on GitHub with a minimal reproduction and steps to reproduce.
support.contributeText: Contributions are welcome via pull requests. Please follow the contribution guidelines in the repository.
support.discussionsText: For general questions, feature requests, or community discussion, please use GitHub Discussions.
support.donateText: If you find the project useful, consider supporting us via donations or sponsorships.

impressum.title: Legal notice
impressum.contentText: The content of this website has been created with the greatest possible care. However, we cannot guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the content. The weather data provided is sourced from various meteorological services and is provided "as is" without warranty.
impressum.linksText: This website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no control. Therefore, we cannot accept any liability for this external content. The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is always responsible for their content.
impressum.openSourceText: Wetterdienst is an open-source project licensed under the MIT License. The source code is available on {link}.

api.title: REST API
api.subtitle: Access weather data programmatically
api.formatsText: All endpoints support multiple output formats via the {format} query parameter:

endpoints.coverage: Discover available providers, networks, resolutions, and parameters
endpoints.stations: Query weather stations by various filters
endpoints.history: Retrieve station history (name, operator, devices, geography, missing data)
endpoints.values: Retrieve observation values for selected stations and parameters
endpoints.interpolate: Interpolate values for a specific location
endpoints.summarize: Get summarized values for a location
endpoints.stripesStations: Get stations for climate stripes visualization
endpoints.stripesValues: Get climate stripes data values with timestamps

meteogram.title: Weather forecast
meteogram.subtitle: DWD MOSMIX forecast — temperature, precipitation and wind
meteogram.aboutText1: The forecast displays a multi-day weather forecast from DWD MOSMIX, a statistical post-processing product that combines numerical weather prediction model output with station observations to produce calibrated hourly forecasts at over 5000 locations across Germany and worldwide.
meteogram.aboutText2: The chart shows temperature & dew point, wind speed & gusts with meteorological wind barbs, precipitation classified by phase (rain / mixed / snow), cloud cover by altitude, and atmospheric pressure. Day/night bands are derived from the station's geographic location. Select any DWD station by name or ID to load its forecast.
meteogram.emptyHint: Search for a station above to generate a forecast
meteogram.searchPlaceholder: Search by name or station ID (e.g. München, 10738)
meteogram.searchHint: Type ≥ 2 characters to search. Use a numeric ID for an exact lookup.

chart.noData: No data available for the selected query

widget.noStation: No station specified. Add {param} to the URL.

stripes.title: Climate stripes
stripes.subtitle: Visualize long-term climate trends with warming/cooling stripes
stripes.aboutText1: Climate stripes (also known as warming stripes) are a data visualization designed to communicate the long-term increase in temperatures. Each stripe represents the average temperature for a single year, with blue indicating cooler years and red indicating warmer years.
stripes.aboutText2: The visualization was created by climate scientist Ed Hawkins and has become an iconic representation of climate change. This tool uses data from the German Weather Service (DWD) observation network.
stripes.emptyHint: Select a station and click Show to create climate stripes

explorer.title: Explorer
explorer.subtitle: Explore stations and observation data interactively
explorer.aboutIntro: The Explorer is an interactive UI for discovering weather stations and retrieving observation data without writing code. Use it to visually pick stations on the map, configure which parameters and time ranges you need, and preview the resulting values. It supports three modes: Station (download measurements from selected stations), Interpolation (estimate values for a geographic point), and Summary (aggregated statistics over an area or point).
explorer.workflow1: Pick a Provider (data source) and a Network.
explorer.workflow2: Choose a Resolution and Dataset and select one or more Parameters.
explorer.workflow3: Switch between modes and select stations on the map or enter a manual location for interpolation/summary.
explorer.workflow4: Provide a Date Range when required (interpolation, summary or high-resolution data).
explorer.workflow5: Open the Data Viewer to preview, inspect stats and export results.
explorer.tip1: High-frequency resolutions (1/5/10 minutes) produce many values — restrict the date range to keep requests reasonable.
explorer.tip2: Use Settings to control unit conversion, result shape (long/wide), and interpolation/summary options.
explorer.tip3: Interpolation and summary both respect nearby station distance and per-parameter distance overrides — adjust them under Advanced Settings for better estimates.
explorer.tip4: If you plan to export, preview the result size in the Data Viewer and reduce stations/parameters or range if needed.
explorer.aboutFooter: For detailed examples and API equivalents, see the documentation or the REST API page.
explorer.unitTargetsHint: Override default target units for specific unit types. Leave empty to use defaults.
explorer.nearbyDistanceHint: Maximum distance to consider nearby stations. If a station is within this distance, its actual value will be used instead of interpolating/summarizing.
explorer.stationDistanceHint: Maximum distance (km) for stations used per parameter. Backend defaults: 40km general, 20km for precipitation_height.
explorer.minGainHint: Minimum gain threshold for adding additional stations. Considers the extra effort against the gain of additional timestamps. (Default: 0.10)
explorer.additionalStationsHint: Number of additional stations to use regardless of gain threshold. (Default: 3)

stationSelection.noneFound: No stations found for the selected parameters

dateRange.label: Date Range
dateRange.errEndAfterStart: End date must be after start date
dateRange.errExceedsLimit: Estimated {count} values exceeds limit of {max}. Please narrow the date range.

dataViewer.emptyHint: Select parameters and stations, then click Show to load data

validation.onlySelect: Only SELECT queries are allowed. Query must start with SELECT or WITH.
validation.noLimit: Query does not contain a LIMIT clause. Large result sets may impact performance.
validation.missingColumns: Query results are missing expected columns: {missing}. Expected columns: {expected}
